untranslatable_reason: This signal requires synthetic option pricing calculations (call_price, put_price, strike_price) and forward price comparisons that cannot be expressed using the allowed indicators. The signal relies on derivatives market data and parity relationships (call - put vs underlying - strike) which are not available in the standard OHLCV-based indicator set. Requires: call prices, put prices, strike prices, and synthetic parity deviation metrics—none of which are in the allowed indicators list.
translation_notes: NATENBERG_DRY_100_0 is a derivatives arbitrage signal based on put-call parity. The core logic involves: (1) Computing synthetic_parity = call_price - put_price and theoretical_parity = underlying_price - strike_price, (2) Measuring deviation |actual - theoretical|, (3) Trading when deviation exceeds transaction cost thresholds. This is fundamentally an options-focused strategy incompatible with spot/futures price-only backtesting. The signal cannot be translated to equity/futures price action alone without access to options market data.
